items.title: Trump urges other nations to help secure shipping through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran had threatened that US-linked oil and energy firms would be "turned into a pile of ashes" if they were hit.

items.title: Danish royals Queen Mary and King Frederik begin Australia visit with Uluru trek
items.summary: Denmark's monarchs have followed in the footsteps of the late Princess Diana and now-King Charles with a visit to a site in Australia's red centre.

items.title: 'Worried for them': Iranian football members en route to Tehran after rescinding asylum
items.summary: Seven members of the team were granted asylum by Australia — now four in total have chosen to return to Iran.

items.title: Amid rationing and shortage fears, here's what Australia's fuel supply looks like in charts
items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen has released 20 per cent of the nation's fuel reserves as experts warn rationing could be on the cards.

items.title: How war in the Middle East is 're-ordering power' and what it means for Australia's alliances
items.summary: The war in the Middle East has signalled changes to the international order, with some saying the system is in "severe crisis".

items.title: Energy minister urges calm over fuel stocks as consumer watchdog scrutinises price increases
items.summary: The government has assured Australians that there is no fuel supply shortage, as the New South Wales government convenes a fuel security roundtable.

items.title: Popular tax discount on electric vehicles could be phased out, just as petrol prices rise
items.summary: Proponents fear ending the Electric Car Discount could unfairly punish families outside major metropolitan areas battling long commutes.

items.title: Punters 'unlikely' to see millions in ticket sales refunded as long-running festival shutters
items.summary: Bluesfest's founder said it was "unlikely" festivalgoers, some of whom spent thousands on tickets and accommodation, would see any refunds.

items.title: North Korea allegedly fires ballistic missiles during US-Korea drills
items.summary: Nuclear-armed North Korea frequently objects to drills by the US and South Korea, calling them "dress rehearsals" for armed aggression.

items.title: Why Australia's famous flying doctors are more important now than ever before
items.summary: "In many communities, the flying doctor isn't just supporting the healthcare system, we are the healthcare system."

items.title: Eleven dead and more than 20 injured in Sudan drone strike
items.summary: Drones have become a key ‌weapon in the war between Sudan's army and the Rapid Support Forces that began in April 2023.

items.title: Matildas to face China in Asian Cup semi-finals as Taiwan defeated in defensive showdown
items.summary: Taiwan played well beyond expectations, keeping China from finding a pair of clear goals until well into stoppage time.

items.title: Kharg Island hit in 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the history of the Middle East'
items.summary: The US military has bombed targets on Iran's Kharg Island, which handles almost all of Iran's crude exports, as US President Donald Trump vowed to hit Iran harder.

items.title: Haiti's first Paralympic skier hits the slopes, as Australia's Ben Tudhope wins bronze
items.summary: Ralf Etienne, who was trapped under an earthquake in Haiti, wants to win gold in 2030.

items.title: Matildas sink North Korea to secure Women’s Asian Cup semi-final berth
items.summary: The 2010 champions will play against either China or Taiwan at Optus Stadium on Tuesday.

items.title: Buying a unit is cheaper than renting in this inner-city spot
items.summary: New research suggests Australians could save hundreds per fortnight by buying rather than renting a unit in certain places.

items.title: Lebanon at 'breaking point', UN warns, as displacement crisis grows
items.summary: Lebanon's civilians bear the brunt as fighting spreads, triggering mass displacement and growing international concern.

items.title: Australian officials pulled from Lebanon, Israel and UAE amid war in the Middle East
items.summary: Non-essential government staff were told to get out of Israel and the UAE earlier on Thursday.

items.title: Their journey has just 'begun': Concerns Iranian players monitored by regime 'agents'
items.summary: Some are worried about the safety of Iranian players who sought asylum in Australia, raising concerns Iran could keep a close eye on them.

items.title: Qantas agrees to $105 million settlement over COVID-19 flight credits
items.summary: The airlines makes no admission of liability under the terms of the settlement, which still requires Federal Court approval.

items.title: Government's 100-million-litre fuel promise amid supply 'panic'
items.summary: The government has released additional fuel into the domestic market, reassuring Australians that "stocks remain very strong".

items.title: Suspect shot dead by security after ramming truck through synagogue in Michigan
items.summary: None of the synagogue's staff or the children at its early childhood centre were injured.

items.title: Iran vows to keep Strait of Hormuz shut in first statement from new Supreme Leader
items.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei says the leverage of closing the Strait of Hormuz should be used and that attacks on Gulf countries will continue.

items.title: Matildas v North Korea: Will this Asian Cup quarter-final see Kerr re-enact first goal magic?
items.summary: With the fate of two players unknown, will the Matildas be able to secure their spot in the 2027 Women's World Cup?
